name: PaliGemma
summary: PaliGemma is Google's open vision-language model that combines a SigLIP vision encoder with a Gemma 2B language decoder, enabling image understanding, captioning, visual question answering, and object detection through text prompts. It processes images at 224x224 resolution and generates text responses describing visual content.
use_when: Multimodal applications requiring image understanding and text generation on consumer GPUs with only 8-16GB VRAM. Visual question answering on documents, charts, and natural images where a lightweight model suffices. Object detection and region captioning through text prompts without requiring separate detection heads.
avoid_when: High-resolution image analysis where 224x224 input crops critical visual details. Production systems requiring strict text-only output guarantees where image inputs could trigger unwanted visual hallucinations. Tasks requiring state-of-the-art vision reasoning where larger models like GPT-4V or Gemini Pro Vision significantly outperform 3B parameter models.
pros: 3B parameter size enables deployment on consumer GPUs and edge devices where 7B+ multimodal models are too memory-intensive
SigLIP vision encoder provides strong zero-shot image understanding and cross-modal alignment without requiring separate image preprocessing pipelines
Gemma decoder offers text generation capabilities with established tokenizer and vocabulary, simplifying integration with existing NLP tooling
Open weights and permissive licensing enable research, fine-tuning, and commercial deployment without usage restrictions or API costs
cons: Fixed 224x224 image resolution limits detail capture for fine-grained visual tasks like small object detection or medical image analysis
3B parameter capacity limits reasoning depth compared to 7B-70B multimodal models, resulting in more frequent factual errors on complex visual questions
Visual hallucinations can occur when the model generates confident but incorrect descriptions of objects not present in the image
Limited fine-tuning recipes and community adapters compared to pure text models, making domain adaptation for specialized vision tasks more challenging

key_hyperparams.name: max_new_tokens
key_hyperparams.note: Maximum text tokens to generate from the image prompt. Increase to 256-512 for detailed captions and long-form visual descriptions.

key_hyperparams.name: image_size
key_hyperparams.note: Input image resolution in pixels. Fixed at 224 for standard PaliGemma checkpoints. Custom fine-tuning can adapt to 448 for higher detail at increased compute cost.

quick_start:
from transformers import AutoProcessor, PaliGemmaForConditionalGeneration
from PIL import Image

model = PaliGemmaForConditionalGeneration.from_pretrained(
    'google/paligemma-3b-pt-224',
    torch_dtype='auto',
    device_map='auto'
)
processor = AutoProcessor.from_pretrained('google/paligemma-3b-pt-224')

image = Image.open('photo.jpg')
prompt = 'caption en'
inputs = processor(text=prompt, images=image, return_tensors='pt').to(model.device)
outputs = model.generate(**inputs, max_new_tokens=128)
print(processor.decode(outputs[0], skip_special_tokens=True))
decision_notes: Choose PaliGemma for lightweight multimodal tasks on consumer GPUs. Use larger multimodal models like GPT-4V for complex visual reasoning.
